market_context: Market sentiment on X/Twitter is cautiously optimistic, with many users discussing potential Fed rate cut signals and strong tech earnings. Pre-market futures for S&P 500 and Nasdaq are up slightly by 0.3% and 0.5% respectively, while notable movers include tech giants like AAPL and NVDA. Major news includes an anticipated economic data release on consumer confidence at 10 AM ET.

predictions.reasoning: Positive sentiment on X/Twitter regarding potential Fed rate cuts is driving bullishness. Pre-market futures for S&P 500 are up 0.3% as reported by Bloomberg data. Consumer confidence data release may further bolster market optimism if results exceed expectations.

NVDA is a notable pre-market mover with a 1.2% gain as per Yahoo Finance. Strong options flow data from X posts indicates heavy call buying for March expiry. Recent earnings optimism in the tech sector adds to the bullish case.

AAPL shows positive pre-market movement of 0.8% according to CNBC data. X sentiment is favorable with mentions of strong product launch expectations. However, uncertainty around broader market reaction to economic data tempers confidence.
